intro: Kotlin compiles to the same JVM bytecode as Java and is fully interoperable with it, so the conversion is largely syntactic. Kotlin's data classes become Java POJOs with getters/setters/equals/hashCode, `val`/`var` declarations become typed Java fields, extension functions become static utility methods, and Kotlin's null safety (`?` types) maps to Java's `Optional` or `@Nullable` annotations. Kotlin coroutines translate to `CompletableFuture` or `ExecutorService`.

diffs.from: Type system distinguishes nullable (T?) from non-null (T) at compile time.
diffs.to: All references nullable by default. NPE is a runtime risk. Use Optional<T> or @Nullable.

diffs.from: data class generates equals, hashCode, copy, toString automatically.
diffs.to: Record (Java 16+) gives similar benefits; POJO requires manual implementation.

diffs.from: kotlinx.coroutines: suspend fun, launch, async, flow.
diffs.to: CompletableFuture, ExecutorService, Project Reactor, or Virtual Threads (Java 21+).

diffs.from: Extension functions add methods to existing types without subclassing.
diffs.to: No extension functions. Use static utility methods or wrapper classes.

diffs.from: val/var, smart casts, when expression, string templates reduce boilerplate.
diffs.to: More verbose. switch vs when, explicit casts, String.format() for interpolation.

examples.title: Data class → Java record
examples.description: Kotlin data class with copy() maps to a Java record for immutable value objects.
examples.fromCode:
data class User(
    val id: Int,
    val name: String,
    val email: String,
    val active: Boolean = true,
)

val alice = User(1, "Alice", "alice@example.com")
val updated = alice.copy(active = false)

examples.title: when expression → switch expression
examples.description: Kotlin's when is an expression; Java's switch expression (Java 14+) covers the same cases.

tips.title: Null-safe (?.) → Optional or null check
tips.body: Kotlin's user?.name becomes Optional.ofNullable(user).map(u -> u.name()).orElse(null) or an explicit if (user != null) guard in Java.

tips.title: suspend fun → CompletableFuture
tips.body: Kotlin suspend functions map to Java CompletableFuture<T>. Kotlin's launch {} becomes CompletableFuture.runAsync(). Flow<T> maps to Reactive Streams or java.util.concurrent.Flow.

tips.title: Extension functions → static utils
tips.body: Kotlin String.truncate() extensions become Java static methods: StringUtils.truncate(String s). Place them in a utility class.

tips.title: data class copy() → record constructor
tips.body: Java records are immutable and have no copy(). Replace alice.copy(active=false) with new User(alice.id(), alice.name(), alice.email(), false).
